name: Porenut
description: Porenut is a relatively obscure Slavic deity known primarily from a single historical source, the 'Life of Saint Otto of Bamberg'. He was worshipped by the Rani tribe, a West Slavic group inhabiting the island of Rügen. Porenut was one of the five heads of the main temple in Korenica, alongside Rugievit, Porevit, and possibly others. The exact nature and function of Porenut are debated due to the limited information available. Some scholars speculate he may have been associated with strength, war, or protection. His five heads might symbolize multiple aspects of his power or represent different roles he fulfilled within the Rani pantheon. The destruction of his idol by Saint Otto's missionaries led to the forced conversion of the Rani people to Christianity. Consequently, detailed knowledge of Porenut and his cult was lost, leaving only fragments and interpretations for modern researchers to piece together. He remains an enigmatic figure, a testament to the rich and complex religious beliefs of pre-Christian Slavic societies.
subtitle: Enigmatic deity of the Rani tribe

keyMyths.title: Destruction of the Idol
keyMyths.description: Saint Otto of Bamberg destroyed Porenut's idol during his missionary work among the Rani. This act symbolized the triumph of Christianity over the pagan beliefs of the tribe.
keyMyths.source: Life of Saint Otto of Bamberg

keyMyths.title: Five-Headed Divinity
keyMyths.description: Porenut's most distinctive feature was his five heads, which likely represented different aspects of his power or roles within the Rani pantheon. The significance of each head remains unknown due to limited historical records.
keyMyths.source: Life of Saint Otto of Bamberg

keyMyths.title: Worship in Korenica
keyMyths.description: Porenut was one of the primary deities worshipped in the temple of Korenica on the island of Rügen. This temple served as a central religious site for the Rani people.
keyMyths.source: Life of Saint Otto of Bamberg

sources.author: Herbordus
sources.work: Life of Saint Otto of Bamberg
sources.description: Primary source mentioning Porenut and his idol's destruction.
sources.text: Primary source mentioning Porenut and his idol's destruction.

sources.author: Helmold of Bosau
sources.description: Provides context for the religious practices of the Rani tribe, though doesn't directly mention Porenut.
